mitigations.mitigation_name: Restrict UbuntuComputer’s ability to run DNS tunneling utilities by removing execute permission from dig and similar DNS clients for the attacker-controlled user account, while leaving wget and ping untouched

args.command: su -s /bin/sh -c 'command -v dig >/dev/null 2>&1 && dig +short example.com || echo DIG_BLOCKED; command -v nslookup >/dev/null 2>&1 && nslookup example.com || echo NSLOOKUP_BLOCKED; command -v host >/dev/null 2>&1 && host example.com || echo HOST_BLOCKED; ping -c 1 -W 1 192.168.1.2 >/dev/null && echo PING_OK; wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1 && echo WGET_OK' agent

python3 - <<'PY'
import os, stat
paths=['/usr/bin/python3','/usr/bin/dig','/usr/bin/nslookup','/usr/bin/host']
for p in paths:
    try:
        s=os.stat(p)
        os.chmod(p, s.st_mode & ~0o111)
        print('CHMOD_OK', p, oct(os.stat(p).st_mode & 0o777))
    except Exception as e:
        print('CHMOD_FAIL', p, e)
PY

su -s /bin/sh -c 'python3 - <<"PY"
print("PYTHON_OK")
PY
; echo PYTHON_EXIT:$?; dig +short example.com 2>/dev/null || echo DIG_BLOCKED; nslookup example.com 2>/dev/null || echo NSLOOKUP_BLOCKED; host example.com 2>/dev/null || echo HOST_BLOCKED; ping -c 1 -W 1 192.168.1.2 >/dev/null && echo PING_OK; wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1 && echo WGET_OK' agent

mitigations.mitigation_name: Enforce a host-level egress policy on UbuntuComputer that blocks outbound DNS traffic except to the approved internal resolver, including UDP/TCP 53 and other DNS tunneling ports, while preserving ICMP and HTTP for validation

args.command: iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p udp --dport 53 -d 203.0.113.1 -j REJECT; iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p tcp --dport 53 -d 203.0.113.1 -j REJECT; iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p udp --dport 8853 -d 203.0.113.1 -j REJECT; iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p udp --dport 5353 -d 203.0.113.1 -j REJECT; iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p tcp --dport 8853 -d 203.0.113.1 -j REJECT; iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p tcp --dport 5353 -d 203.0.113.1 -j REJECT; iptables -S OUTPUT

iptables -P OUTPUT ACCEPT; iptables -F OUTPUT; iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p udp -m multiport --dports 53,8853,5353 -d 0.0.0.0/0 ! -d 192.168.1.1 -j REJECT; iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p tcp -m multiport --dports 53,8853,5353 -d 0.0.0.0/0 ! -d 192.168.1.1 -j REJECT; iptables -S OUTPUT; ping -c 1 -W 2 192.168.1.2; wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1; echo HTTP_OK

iptables -F OUTPUT; iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p udp -m multiport --dports 53,8853,5353 -m iprange ! --dst-range 192.168.1.1-192.168.1.1 -j REJECT; iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p tcp -m multiport --dports 53,8853,5353 -m iprange ! --dst-range 192.168.1.1-192.168.1.1 -j REJECT; iptables -S OUTPUT; ping -c 1 -W 2 192.168.1.2; wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1; echo HTTP_OK

mitigations.mitigation_name: Restrict the attacker account on UbuntuComputer from executing DNS tunneling tools and scripting helpers used for exfiltration, while leaving wget and ping operational

args.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import os, stat
for p in ['/usr/bin/dig','/usr/bin/python3.12','/bin/nc.openbsd','/usr/bin/perl','/usr/bin/socat1']:
    st=os.stat(p)
    print(oct(st.st_mode & 0o777), p)
PY

python3 -c 'import os; print("PY_OK", os.path.exists("/usr/bin/dig"), os.access("/usr/bin/dig", os.X_OK), os.access("/usr/bin/perl", os.X_OK), os.access("/bin/nc.openbsd", os.X_OK), os.access("/usr/bin/socat1", os.X_OK))'; ping -c 1 -W 1 192.168.1.2 >/dev/null && echo PING_OK; wget -q -T 3 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1 && echo HTTP_OK
